step_by_step_reasoning: Based on the transcript, acoustic sensors on fire hydrants (IoT devices) push data daily. This ingestion path starts with the IoT devices sending files through API Gateway, which authenticates and persists them in S3. A separate daily batch process runs on ECS to execute processor-intensive acoustic algorithms finding correlations. The persistent correlations are then analyzed by a SageMaker ML endpoint to classify leak probability. This score is delivered to human analysts who qualify the leak.

graph.name: Muller Water Products Intelligent Water Platform
graph.notes: Ingests daily acoustic data from smart hydrants, processes with ECS containers, and uses SageMaker to classify leak probabilities for analyst review.

nodes.name: Smart Fire Hydrants
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Acoustic noise signal files recorded daily on pipe networks.

nodes.name: Ingestion API
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Handles security and authentication for incoming IoT files.

nodes.name: Acoustic Data Store
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Raw acoustic signals and correlation data.

nodes.name: Correlation Algorithms
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Processor-intensive microservices running daily batch correlation algorithms.

nodes.name: Leak Classifier
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: ML endpoint that assigns leak probabilities to persistent correlations.

nodes.name: Water Analyst Team
nodes.notes: Reviews leak scores and coordinates with water utilities for field investigation.

edges.notes: Pulls daily files for batch processing
